exemplar.title: How does age group in Petaling Jaya, Selangor influence the level of awareness, in terms of knowledge and practice, on the disposal of batteries to reduce E-waste in order to prevent groundwater pollution?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does age group in Petaling Jaya, Selangor influence the level of awareness, in terms of knowledge and practice, on the disposal of batteries to reduce E-waste in order to prevent groundwater pollution?

subject.title: Environmental systems and societies (ESS - Old)
subject.description: IB Environmental Systems and Societies (ESS) examines environmental challenges through an interdisciplinary approach combining natural sciences with social and economic perspectives. Students study ecosystems, biodiversity, resource management, pollution, climate change, and sustainability using systems thinking and collaborative investigation. The course develops environmental literacy and critical thinking skills essential for understanding and addressing complex environmental issues facing our planet.

strengths: Report structure follows a logical RQâMethodâResultsâEvaluation sequence throughout
Consistent use of appropriate technical terminology
Embedded figures and tables near discussion sections
weaknesses: Long calculation tables and narrative digressions interrupt reading flow
Logical jumps between analysis and evaluation hinder coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication is generally logical (band 2â3) and well organized (band 3), but occasional lack of concision and minor coherence issues place it at band 2 overall. A score of 2 reflects these mixed strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Clear, focused research question with specific variables and geographical context
Sustained, quantitative local and international environmental issue context
Comprehensive explanation of connections between age-related awareness and groundwater contamination
weaknesses: The research question wording is somewhat wordy
Minor suggestions to tighten causal-chain phrasing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the introduction firmly in the 5â6 band: the student formulates a coherent, focused question, provides strong local e-waste statistics and sustains contextualisation through citations. A few minor wordiness issues keep it from full perfection, so a score of 5 (rather than 6) reflects excellent performance with small room for concision improvement.

strengths: Well-detailed, repeatable questionnaire method with clear statistical plan
Justified quota sampling strategy tied to population strata
weaknesses: Omission of data-cleaning procedures for missing responses
Control variable section lacks mention of key confounders (e.g., education, gender)
Ethical/risk discussion omits data security and emotional discomfort
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The method and sampling strands are strong (band 5) and well justified, but the ethics strand sits at band 4 due to missing considerations (data security, broader risk assessment). These mid-band omissions justify an overall holistic score of 4 rather than 5.

strengths: Identifies key strengths, weaknesses and limitations of the method (e.g., large sample, self-report bias)
Evaluates findings in context of literature and generalisability
Offers meaningful improvements and further research linked to weaknesses
weaknesses: Does not address instrument reliability measures (e.g., Cronbachâs alpha)
Statistical-assumption tests (e.g., Leveneâs) are not reported
Improvement suggestions lack step-wise implementation details
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student evaluates conclusions and methods well (band 4) and proposes meaningful extensions (band 3), situating performance in the 3â4 band range. The absence of full reliability evaluation and deeper statistical diagnostics supports a holistic score of 4.

strengths: Correct use of means, standard deviations and one-way ANOVA with explicit pattern interpretation
Appropriate charts for all primary variables
Conclusions directly mirror statistical outcomes and note nuanced attitudeâbehavior gaps
weaknesses: Missing gender percentages in demographic tables and some practice-item percentages
Incomplete axis labels and units on figures
Mixed notation for statistical formulas and missing summary rows
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While data presentation, analysis and conclusion drawing are conceptually correct (band 5 strands), multiple omissions in tables, labelling, notation and completeness indicate it falls short of flawless execution. A holistic score of 4 reflects strong but not fully comprehensive performance.

strengths: Proposed Circu Li-ion up-cycling application tightly linked to findings
Balanced, justified evaluation of solution strengths (speed, safety, cost) and weaknesses (capital, expertise)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both application proposal and solution evaluation meet all top-band descriptors with clear linkage to data and comprehensive trade-off analysis. Full marks (3/3) are therefore warranted.

strandSelections.reasoning: Environmental, statistical and methodological terms (e-waste, ANOVA, Likert, quota sampling) are used correctly.  Nevertheless, repetitive phrasing and occasional colloquialisms (âbig scale of the problemâ) reduce concision, matching descriptor level 2.

The student reviews how findings align with literature, considers generalisability and highlights the attitudeâbehaviour gap, showing evaluation beyond restatement.  Some aspectsâsuch as socioeconomic confoundersâare only briefly acknowledged, leaving minor omissions that keep it in the 3â4 band.

The conclusion directly mirrors statistical outcomes (p = 0.0125) and numerical means, clearly supporting or rejecting hypotheses.  It recognises nuance (awareness â  practice) and remains tightly linked to data, fulfilling the descriptor of full data support.

Means, standard deviations and a one-way ANOVA are correctly executed and interpreted.  Patterns (e.g., seniors highest awareness) are explicitly identified.  Although minor calculation slips could exist, the analysis covers all collected variables thoroughly and without conceptual error.

The student proposes improving recycling infrastructure, incentive programs and behavioural observations, which are meaningful and linked to identified weaknesses.  They address several major issues but stop short of a detailed, step-wise redesign, fitting mid-band performance.

A step-by-step method (questionnaire construction, pilot test, distribution, statistical treatment) is provided, including the exact number of questions, Likert scaling, quota targets and planned analyses (mean, SD, ANOVA).  Another assessor could repeat the investigation with little ambiguity; hence the method is appropriate and sufficiently detailed for collecting relevant data.

Connections between the environmental issue (groundwater pollution via e-waste batteries) and the chosen social variable (age-related awareness) are explicitly explained in several passages.  The student shows how awareness influences battery disposal, which in turn affects leachate formation and mineral recovery.  The chain of reasoning is explicit and largely complete, warranting the top-band description level.

The student formulates a specific, single-sentence research question that clearly identifies the independent variable (age group) and dependent aspects (knowledge and practice of battery disposal).  The phrasing is focused, avoids unnecessary scope creep and embeds the geographical context (Petaling Jaya, Selangor).  Minor wordiness remains, but the intent and boundaries of the enquiry are unmistakable, justifying placement in the 5â6 band rather than 3â4.

Strengths (speed, safety, cost), weaknesses (high capital, expertise, infrastructure) are explicitly evaluated, covering all relevant angles for the proposed solution; hence the top score in this strand.

The report follows a clear, logical sequence (RQ â Intro â Method â Results â Analysis â Evaluation) with headings and numbering that facilitate navigation across 25 pages.  Figures and tables are embedded near discussion, indicating sustained organisation.

Strengths (large sample, cost efficiency), weaknesses (self-report bias), and limitations (sampling bias, behaviour not observed) are identified and explained.  However, instrument reliability and potential statistical errors are not discussed, so not *all* limitations are covered.

The introduction discusses e-waste quantities in Malaysia, toxic components of batteries and the threat of groundwater contamination, providing quantitative local data (365 000 t e-waste; 2 459 t household) and international comparisons.  This contextualisation is sustained through citations and links the local problem to broader sustainability concerns, demonstrating a well-developed environmental issue context.

The student justifies quota sampling by explaining representativeness across three age strata and cites a methodology source.  Population proportions and minimum cell sizes are stated, demonstrating a reasoned choice rather than a mere description, satisfying the justification requirement.

Ethical safeguards (voluntary participation, anonymity, bilingual survey) and the sole identified risk (question misinterpretation) are outlined along with mitigation (pilot test).  While adequate, the treatment is brief and omits other plausible risks such as data security or emotional discomfort, so the description is complete but not fully comprehensive.

All primary data (demographics, nine knowledge items, five practice items and a multiple-choice source question) are tabulated and accompanied by clearly labelled bar or pie charts.  No relevant numeric data are omitted, and visual choices are appropriate to variable type.

The Circu Li-ion robotic up-cycling proposal is directly linked to findings (need for efficient battery recycling) and justified through efficiency, safety and labour advantages.  The rationale demonstrates clear linkage to data, warranting full marks in this 0â3 strand.

Argument flow is generally logical, but some long calculation tables and narrative digressions interrupt reading ease.  Minor jumps (e.g., analysis before full data display) make sections harder to follow, indicating a mainly logical but occasionally difficult presentation.

annotations.comment: The research question is specific and contextually grounded, but its phrasing is somewhat wordy. Consider simplifying for concision, e.g., remove âin order to preventâ and focus on key variables to improve readability.
Introduction provides strong local e-waste statistics and toxicological context, linking to groundwater risk. This sustained contextualization demonstrates thorough environmental issue understanding.
The link between age-related awareness and environmental outcomes is well articulated, but consider clarifying the causal chain by briefly explaining how practice influences leachate formation.
The hypothesis is logically derived from prior literature, but the age brackets differ from the research question wording. Ensure consistency (18â24 vs 18â35) to avoid confusion.
The materials list correctly identifies key resources, but listing âelectronic gadgetsâ is too broad. Specify which devicesâ batteries (e.g., smartphones) to align with study scope.
Variables table is clear and justified, but control variables section should mention potential confounders (education, gender) to strengthen design justification.
Ethical considerations mention confidentiality, but omit data security measures. Consider specifying storage protocols and data retention policies to ensure full ethical coverage.
Survey interface figure is clear and bilingual, supporting accessibility. Including a consent statement enhances ethical transparency.
Method steps are clearly enumerated but lack mention of how missing responses will be handled in analysis. Address data cleaning procedures for completeness.
Quota sampling is well justified with demographic rationale, but clarify how population proportions were obtained (e.g., census data citation) to strengthen justification.
Figure 5 interpretation correctly identifies key sources, but discuss potential sampling bias of media-savvy respondents to strengthen evaluation.
Demographic Table 3 omits gender percentages for adults and seniors. Including these percentages would complete data presentation and avoid omissions.
Interpretation of Figure 2 effectively summarizes key educational background trends, but could quantify variance to highlight statistical significance.
Calculation formulas are correctly stated, but clarify that $$f$$ denotes frequency of responses and N equals total respondents for full comprehension.
Example calculation for standard deviation is thorough, but use correct notation: replace denominator $$N-1$$ with âdegrees of freedomâ explicitly to improve statistical accuracy.
Data tables in Section B present means and standard deviations clearly, but adding a summary row for total respondents per question would enhance completeness.
Interpretive list items correctly identify patterns, but some bullet points conflate knowledge and practice findings. Separate categories to maintain analytical clarity.
Section C practices data are well-tabulated, but percentages for Adults in C10âC12 group are missing; complete these fields to avoid inconsistent presentation.
Figures for mean scores in Section C correctly reflect data but lack axis labels and units. Ensure charts include complete labels for standalone clarity.
Summary list on page 15 concisely encapsulates practices results; linking back explicitly to research question closure would reinforce coherence.
Presentation of multiple-choice results is clear, but consider adding comparative commentary on how source usage affects awareness trends.
Data analysis figure is informative, but do not use âcorrelationâ when describing ANOVA results; ANOVA tests differences between means, not correlations.
Statistical analysis notes ANOVA assumptions but does not report homogeneity or normality tests. Including Leveneâs test results would strengthen validity evaluation.
ANOVA summary table clearly reports MS, F, and p-value. Excellent inclusion of F critical value to contextualize significance threshold.
Conclusion aligns with data and correctly rejects the null hypothesis; it also notes the attitudeâbehavior gap, reflecting nuanced interpretation.
Discussion acknowledges broader applicability and limitations; well-linked to literature. Consider adding quantitative caveats (e.g., effect size) to deepen evaluation.
Method evaluation highlights survey strengths and weaknesses effectively; to fully meet criterion, discuss instrument reliability measures (e.g., Cronbachâs alpha).
Application section effectively proposes Circu Li-ion solution and evaluates trade-offs. Strengths and weaknesses are balanced and justified by findings.
Communication is generally logical but sections sometimes jump from analysis to evaluation. Reordering to conclude analysis fully before moving on would enhance coherence.
Bibliography entries show relevant sources but formatting is inconsistent (mix of styles). Apply consistent citation style (e.g., APA) for professionalism.
